sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit 50/50 for the same reason it broke through in 2011: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jonathan Levine keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: A Little Bit of Heaven
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If 50/50 clicked for you, A Little Bit of Heaven (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after 50/50?
faq.answer: Try A Little Bit of Heaven, Another Year, Jump In!—each shares DNA with 50/50 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is 50/50?
faq.answer: 50/50 (2011) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is 50/50 worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
